verbatim_text: And I think one thing here is we don't have a really good sense of what it means for an explanation to actually be useful. I think it might be worth working backwards with people in these high stakes domains like experts and see what explanations are most useful for people. OK, so I'm also excited to explore applications of self models beyond interpretability. I think there's all kinds of things we want to optimize for that kind of fall under this self consistency framework. So for example, in safety and alignment, we might want models that can articulate their own behaviors, their own edge cases or their own jail breaks. And this could offer us a path towards models that could read team themselves for keeping models robust to prompt paraphrase for keeping them making sure they're not sycophantic. These can be addressed by training language models to be invariant to semantics preserving perturbations to their inputs. And then continual learning, you can think of that as actually also a self consistency issue. We want the model to be consistent under under updates. So whenever we train it on a newly learned fact, we want it to consistently adjust its behavior on all downstream implications of that fact.
